title: सोमवार व्रत कथा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सोमवार व्रत कथा ॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय॥

॥ व्रत विधि ॥
सोमवार का व्रत भगवान शिव को समर्पित है। श्रावण मास के सोमवार विशेष फलदायी हैं। प्रातः स्नान करके शिवलिंग पर जल, दूध, बिल्वपत्र चढ़ाएँ। एक समय भोजन करें। सायंकाल कथा सुनें।

॥ कथा ॥
एक समय की बात है, काशीपुरी में एक धनी साहूकार रहता था।
उसके यहाँ किसी वस्तु की कमी नहीं थी, परन्तु सन्तान न होने से
वह बहुत दुःखी था। पुत्र प्राप्ति के लिए वह प्रत्येक सोमवार को
शिवालय में जाकर भगवान शिव और पार्वती की पूजा करता था॥

एक दिन माता पार्वती ने भगवान शिव से कहा — हे नाथ!
इस साहूकार पर कृपा कीजिए। यह बड़ा भक्त है।
भगवान शिव बोले — हे पार्वती! इस संसार में सबको अपने
कर्मों का फल भोगना पड़ता है। किन्तु तुम्हारे कहने पर
मैं इसे पुत्र रत्न का वरदान देता हूँ,
किन्तु यह पुत्र केवल बारह वर्ष ही जीवित रहेगा॥

साहूकार ने यह वरदान सुन लिया। कालान्तर में उसके
एक सुन्दर पुत्र उत्पन्न हुआ। जब पुत्र ग्यारह वर्ष का हुआ,
तो उसे काशी पढ़ने भेजा गया।
साहूकार ने मामा को बुलाकर कहा कि इसे काशी ले जाओ
और मार्ग में यज्ञ कराते हुए जाना॥

मामा भानजे को लेकर काशी की ओर चले। मार्ग में एक नगर आया।
वहाँ के राजा की कन्या का विवाह हो रहा था। किन्तु वर काना था।
वर पक्ष वालों ने सोचा कि इस सुन्दर ब्राह्मण बालक को
विवाह मण्डप में बैठा दें, विवाह के बाद काने वर को भेज देंगे॥

ऐसा ही किया। किन्तु राजकन्या ने उस सुन्दर बालक के
गले में वरमाला डाल दी। इस प्रकार वह बालक विवाहित हो गया।
किन्तु उसने सत्य बता दिया और अपनी पत्नी को वहीं छोड़कर
मामा के साथ काशी चला गया॥

काशी पहुँचकर दोनों ने यज्ञ कराये। मामा-भानजे शिवालय में
नित्य पूजा करते। बारहवें वर्ष का अन्तिम दिन आया।
शिव कृपा से उस दिन भगवान शिव स्वयं प्रकट हुए।
बालक की भक्ति और सोमवार व्रत के प्रभाव से
भगवान शिव ने प्रसन्न होकर उसकी आयु बढ़ा दी
और दीर्घायु का वरदान दिया॥

साहूकार का पुत्र दीर्घायु होकर सुखपूर्वक जीवन व्यतीत करने लगा।
उसकी पत्नी भी उसके पास आ गई। दोनों ने आजीवन सोमवार
का व्रत किया और अन्त में शिवलोक को प्राप्त हुए॥

॥ कथा सार ॥
जो भक्त नियमपूर्वक सोलह सोमवार या श्रावण के सोमवार व्रत करता है,
भगवान शिव उस पर प्रसन्न होकर सभी मनोकामनाएँ पूर्ण करते हैं।
सन्तान, स्वास्थ्य, दीर्घायु और मोक्ष प्रदान करते हैं।

॥ आरती ॥
जय शिव ओंकारा, ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा।
ब्रह्मा विष्णु सदाशिव, अर्द्धाङ्गी धारा। ॐ जय शिव ओंकारा॥

॥ इति श्री सोमवार व्रत कथा सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Somvar Vrat Katha ||

Om Namah Shivaya.

|| Vrat Vidhi ||
Somvar Ka Vrat Bhagwan Shiva Ko Samarpit Hai. Shravan Mas Ke Somvar Vishesh Phaldayi Hain. Pratah Snan Karke Shivalinga Par Jal, Dudh, Bilvapatra Chadhayein. Ek Samay Bhojan Karen. Sayankal Katha Sunen.

|| Katha ||
Ek Samay Ki Baat Hai, Kashipuri Mein Ek Dhani Sahukar Rahta Tha.
Uske Yahan Kisi Vastu Ki Kami Nahin Thi, Parantu Santan Na Hone Se
Vah Bahut Dukhee Tha. Putra Prapti Ke Liye Vah Pratyek Somvar Ko
Shivalaya Mein Jakar Bhagwan Shiva Aur Parvati Ki Puja Karta Tha.

Ek Din Mata Parvati Ne Bhagwan Shiva Se Kaha — He Nath!
Is Sahukar Par Kripa Kijiye. Yah Bada Bhakta Hai.
Bhagwan Shiva Bole — He Parvati! Is Sansar Mein Sabko Apne
Karmon Ka Phal Bhogna Padta Hai. Kintu Tumhare Kahne Par
Main Ise Putra Ratna Ka Vardan Deta Hun,
Kintu Yah Putra Keval Barah Varsh Hi Jivit Rahega.

Sahukar Ne Yah Vardan Sun Liya. Kalantar Mein Uske
Ek Sundar Putra Utpanna Hua. Jab Putra Gyarah Varsh Ka Hua,
To Use Kashi Padhne Bheja Gaya.
Sahukar Ne Mama Ko Bulakar Kaha Ki Ise Kashi Le Jao
Aur Marg Mein Yagya Karate Hue Jana.

Mama Bhaanje Ko Lekar Kashi Ki Or Chale. Marg Mein Ek Nagar Aaya.
Vahan Ke Raja Ki Kanya Ka Vivah Ho Raha Tha. Kintu Var Kana Tha.
Var Paksh Walon Ne Socha Ki Is Sundar Brahman Balak Ko
Vivah Mandap Mein Baitha Den, Vivah Ke Baad Kane Var Ko Bhej Denge.

Aisa Hi Kiya. Kintu Rajkanya Ne Us Sundar Balak Ke
Gale Mein Varmala Daal Di. Is Prakar Vah Balak Vivahit Ho Gaya.
Kintu Usne Satya Bata Diya Aur Apni Patni Ko Vahin Chhodkar
Mama Ke Sath Kashi Chala Gaya.

Kashi Pahunchkar Donon Ne Yagya Karaye. Mama-Bhaanje Shivalaya Mein
Nitya Puja Karte. Barahven Varsh Ka Antim Din Aaya.
Shiva Kripa Se Us Din Bhagwan Shiva Svayam Prakat Hue.
Balak Ki Bhakti Aur Somvar Vrat Ke Prabhav Se
Bhagwan Shiva Ne Prasanna Hokar Uski Aayu Badha Di
Aur Dirghayu Ka Vardan Diya.

Sahukar Ka Putra Dirghayu Hokar Sukhpurvak Jivan Vyatit Karne Laga.
Uski Patni Bhi Uske Pas Aa Gayi. Donon Ne Aajivan Somvar
Ka Vrat Kiya Aur Ant Mein Shivalok Ko Prapt Hue.

|| Katha Sar ||
Jo Bhakta Niyampurvak Solah Somvar Ya Shravan Ke Somvar Vrat Karta Hai,
Bhagwan Shiva Us Par Prasanna Hokar Sabhi Manokamnayein Purn Karte Hain.
Santan, Swasthya, Dirghayu Aur Moksha Pradan Karte Hain.

|| Aarti ||
Jai Shiv Onkara, Om Jai Shiv Onkara.
Brahma Vishnu Sadashiva, Ardhangi Dhara. Om Jai Shiv Onkara.

|| Iti Shri Somvar Vrat Katha Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
सोमवार व्रत कथा भगवान शिव की कृपा और भक्ति की महिमा का वर्णन करती है।

काशी के एक सन्तानहीन धनी साहूकार ने प्रत्येक सोमवार शिवालय में शिव-पार्वती की पूजा की। माता पार्वती की अनुशंसा पर भगवान शिव ने पुत्र का वरदान दिया, किन्तु आयु केवल बारह वर्ष रखी।

पुत्र को मामा के साथ काशी भेजा गया। मार्ग में एक राजकन्या ने उसे वर मानकर वरमाला पहना दी। काशी में सोमवार व्रत और शिव पूजा के प्रभाव से बारहवें वर्ष के अन्तिम दिन भगवान शिव प्रकट हुए और बालक को दीर्घायु का वरदान दिया।

कथा का सन्देश है कि नियमित सोमवार व्रत और भगवान शिव की भक्ति से सन्तान, स्वास्थ्य, दीर्घायु और अन्ततः मोक्ष प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Traditional fasting story for Monday (Somvar) dedicated to Lord Shiva
Observing 16 Mondays (Solah Somvar) vrat fulfills all wishes
Invokes children, health, longevity, and prosperity
Especially powerful during Shravan (monsoon) month Mondays
Listening to the Katha is integral part of the Somvar Vrat
Leads to Shivaloka through lifelong Monday fasting practice
description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Somvar Vrat Katha (Monday Fasting Story) (सोमवार व्रत कथा) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Shiva Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to grant children, health, longevity, and prosperity and lead to Shivaloka through lifelong Monday fasting practice. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month to invoke divine grace. At 15 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 12 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Somvar Vrat Katha (Monday Fasting Story) in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

Mahamrityunjaya jaap with Somvar vrat benefits

seoFaqs.question: What is the Somvar Vrat Katha and what is its central story?
seoFaqs.answer: The Somvar Vrat Katha is the traditional fasting narrative recited on Mondays (Somvar) by devotees observing the Shiva-vrata. The central story tells of a wealthy childless merchant of Kashi (Banaras) who worshipped Shiva and Parvati every Monday in the Shivalaya. Moved by his devotion and Parvati's intercession, Shiva granted him a son with the condition that the child would only live for twelve years. When the boy was eleven, his maternal uncle took him to Kashi for studies, performing yajnas along the way. En route, the boy was substituted in a princess's wedding (the original groom being kana, one-eyed) but truthfully revealed the deception. The princess kept her vow to him. In Kashi, on the final day of his twelfth year, Shiva was moved by the boy's devotion and the merit of Somvar Vrata to extend his life and grant longevity. The boy and princess returned and lived blessed lives. (Sources: Shiva Purana Vrata Khanda; Traditional Vrat Katha Sangrah.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Solah Somvar Vrat and how is it different from Shravan Somvar?
seoFaqs.answer: Solah Somvar (sixteen Mondays) is a sankalpa-vrata where the devotee fasts for sixteen consecutive Mondays starting on a chosen auspicious Monday, performing Shiva puja and reciting this katha each week. It is especially recommended for unmarried persons seeking marriage, childless couples seeking progeny, and those seeking specific desire-fulfilment. The vow concludes with an udyapan ceremony on the seventeenth Monday with brahmin-bhojan, daan and Shiva katha. Shravan Somvar (Sawan Mondays) refers specifically to the Mondays falling within the holy month of Shravan (July-August in the Amanta calendar), which numbers four or five Mondays and is considered the most powerful period for Shiva worship — chanting Sri Rudram, performing Rudrabhishekam, offering bilva leaves and the Shivalingapooja. Shravan Somvar is calendrical; Solah Somvar is a personal sankalpa. (Sources: Shiva Purana; Drikpanchang Sawan Somvar.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the prescribed vrat vidhi for Somvar Vrat?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional vrat vidhi prescribes: (1) Pratah snana — early morning bath and donning clean (preferably white) clothes; (2) Sankalpa — formal vow before the Shivalinga at home or temple specifying the purpose; (3) Shivalinga abhisheka — abhisheka with water, milk (panchamrita), Ganga jal, and offering of bilva-patra (bel leaves), white flowers (datura, akanda), bhang, dhatura fruits, and akshata; (4) Mantra japa — Om Namah Shivaya 108 times, Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra 108 times, and Rudra Gayatri; (5) Eka-bhukta upavasa — single meal of phalahar (fruits, milk, kuttu) before sunset, no grains or salt; (6) Sayam-kal Somvar Vrat Katha shravana — listening to or reciting this katha at twilight; (7) Shiva-aarti (Jai Shiv Onkara) and prasad distribution. (Sources: Shiva Purana Vrata Khanda; Drikpanchang Somvar vrat vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Somvar Vrat traditionally observed?
seoFaqs.answer: Somvar Vrat can be undertaken on any Monday of the year, but specific Mondays are considered exceptionally auspicious. Shravan Somvar (the four or five Mondays in the holy month of Shravan, July-August) is the most powerful — Shravan being the month when Samudra Manthana occurred and Shiva drank the Halahala poison, becoming Nilakantha. Solah Somvar (sixteen consecutive Mondays as a sankalpa-vrata) is undertaken for specific desires. Maha Shivaratri (Phalguna Krishna Chaturdashi) — even when not falling on Monday — is the supreme Shiva festival including ratri-jagarana. Pradosh Vrata (trayodashi tithi twilight) when falling on Monday becomes Soma-pradosh, which is the most powerful Pradosh of the lunar fortnight. The Karkataka Sankranti Dakshinayana is also observed with Somvar fasts. (Sources: Drikpanchang Sawan Somvar; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Somvar Vrat Katha promise to devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: The katha-saara (story conclusion) declares that those who observe Somvar Vrat with discipline — especially Solah Somvar or the Shravan Somvar fasts — receive the fulfilment of all desires (sabhi manokamnayein purn karte hain) by Shiva's grace, including: santana (children) for the childless, swasthya (health) and dirghayu (longevity) for the ailing and aged, vivaha-yoga (timely marriage) for the unmarried, dampatya-sukha (conjugal harmony) for the married, dhana-samriddhi (wealth-prosperity) for the financially struggling, removal of grah-dosha (planetary afflictions especially Chandra and Shani), protection from akala-mrityu (untimely death — paralleling the Maha Mrityunjaya promise), and ultimately moksha through Shivaloka-prapti at the end of life as the merchant's family attained. (Sources: Shiva Purana phalashruti; Traditional Vrat Katha Sangrah.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Somvar Vrat Katha relate to other Shiva vratas and texts?
seoFaqs.answer: Somvar Vrat is the weekly Shiva vrata par excellence, complemented by Pradosh Vrata (twice-monthly twilight worship of the trayodashi tithi), Maha Shivaratri (annual all-night Shiva worship in Phalguna), and the Shravan-mas vow (full month of Shiva-bhakti in July-August). The katha tradition recited on each of these days draws from the Shiva Purana, Linga Purana and Skanda Purana vrata-khandas. The Sri Rudram (Namakam-Chamakam) of Yajur Veda Krishna Yajus 4.5/4.7 is the principal Vedic Shiva text recited during Rudrabhishekam. Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra is the apamrityu-nivarana japa. Together with Solah Somvar Vrata, Pradosh Vrata, Maha Shivaratri jagarana and Shravan parayana, these form the complete Shaiva annual upasana cycle for householders. (Sources: Shiva Purana Vrata Khanda; Drikpanchang Shaiva calendar.)
